questions.markScheme:
1. Factorise denominator: x² - 4 = (x - 2)(x + 2) (M1)
2. Set up: (2x + 3)/((x - 2)(x + 2)) = A/(x - 2) + B/(x + 2) (M1)
3. 2x + 3 = A(x + 2) + B(x - 2); substituting x = 2 gives 7 = 4A so A = 7/4; substituting x = -2 gives -1 = -4B so B = 1/4 (A1)
4. Answer: 7/(4(x - 2)) + 1/(4(x + 2)) (A1)

questions.text:
The polynomial f(x) = 2x³ - 5x² - 4x + 3.

(a) Show that (x - 3) is a factor of f(x).
(b) Hence factorise f(x) completely.
Solve the equation 2 sin²θ + sin θ - 1 = 0 for 0° ≤ θ ≤ 360°.
questions.markScheme:
1. Let u = sin θ: 2u² + u - 1 = 0 (M1)
2. Factorise: (2u - 1)(u + 1) = 0 (M1)
3. u = 1/2 or u = -1 (A1)
4. sin θ = 1/2 gives θ = 30°, 150° (A1)
5. sin θ = -1 gives θ = 270° (A1)

questions.text:
The curve C has equation y = 3eˣ - 2x.

(a) Find dy/dx.
(b) Find the exact coordinates of the stationary point on C.
(c) Determine whether this stationary point is a maximum or a minimum. Justify your answer.
questions.markScheme:
(a)
1. dy/dx = 3eˣ - 2 (M1A1)

(b)
2. At stationary point: 3eˣ - 2 = 0, so eˣ = 2/3 (M1)
3. x = ln(2/3), y = 3(2/3) - 2ln(2/3) = 2 - 2ln(2/3) (A1)

(c)
4. d²y/dx² = 3eˣ (M1)
5. At x = ln(2/3), d²y/dx² = 3 × (2/3) = 2 > 0, so the stationary point is a minimum (A1)

questions.text: Find the exact value of ∫₁⁴ (3/√x + 2x) dx.

The line L passes through the points A(1, 3) and B(5, 11).

(a) Find the equation of L in the form y = mx + c.
(b) The line M is perpendicular to L and passes through the midpoint of AB. Find the equation of M.

A geometric series has first term a = 6 and common ratio r = 0.8.

(a) Find the sum of the first 10 terms. Give your answer to 3 significant figures.
(b) Explain why this geometric series has a sum to infinity, and find the value of S∞.
Solve the equation 5²ˣ⁺¹ = 3ˣ⁺², giving your answer in the form x = (a ln b + c ln d)/(e ln f + g ln h), where a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h are integers.
questions.markScheme:
1. Take natural logarithms of both sides: (2x + 1)ln 5 = (x + 2)ln 3 (M1)
2. Expand: 2x ln 5 + ln 5 = x ln 3 + 2 ln 3 (M1)
3. Collect x terms: 2x ln 5 - x ln 3 = 2 ln 3 - ln 5 (M1)
4. x(2 ln 5 - ln 3) = 2 ln 3 - ln 5, so x = (2 ln 3 - ln 5)/(2 ln 5 - ln 3) (A1)

questions.text: Use the Newton-Raphson method with x₀ = 2 to find the root of f(x) = x³ - 3x - 5, correct to 3 decimal places. Show two iterations of the method.

papers.title: AQA A-Level Mathematics Paper 2 Statistics and Mechanics 2023

questions.text:
A factory produces light bulbs. The lifetimes of the bulbs are normally distributed with a mean of 1200 hours and a standard deviation of 80 hours.

Find the probability that a randomly selected light bulb has a lifetime of more than 1350 hours.

A researcher wants to investigate the number of hours students spend on homework per week.

(a) Explain what is meant by a sampling frame.
(b) Describe one advantage of using a stratified sample rather than a simple random sample for this investigation.
questions.markScheme:
(a)
1. A sampling frame is a list of all members of the population from which the sample is drawn (1)

(b)
2. A stratified sample ensures proportional representation of different subgroups / strata, such as different year groups (1)
3. This reduces sampling bias / gives a more representative sample than a simple random sample where some groups might be over- or under-represented (1)

questions.text:
The heights, in cm, of 50 plants were recorded. The data is summarised in the table below.

Σx = 1250, Σx² = 32,150, n = 50

(a) Calculate the mean height.
(b) Calculate the standard deviation of the heights.
questions.markScheme:
(a)
1. Mean = Σx/n = 1250/50 = 25 cm (M1A1)

(b)
2. Variance = Σx²/n - (Σx/n)² = 32150/50 - 25² = 643 - 625 = 18 (M1)
3. Standard deviation = √18 = 4.24 cm (3 s.f.) (A1)

questions.text:
A bag contains 5 red balls and 3 blue balls. Two balls are drawn at random without replacement.

(a) Draw a tree diagram to show all possible outcomes.
(b) Find the probability that both balls are the same colour.
questions.markScheme:
(a) Tree diagram with correct probabilities (M1):
First draw: P(R) = 5/8, P(B) = 3/8
Second draw given R first: P(R) = 4/7, P(B) = 3/7
Second draw given B first: P(R) = 5/7, P(B) = 2/7

(b)
2. P(both same) = P(RR) + P(BB) (M1)
3. = (5/8 × 4/7) + (3/8 × 2/7) (M1)
4. = 20/56 + 6/56 = 26/56 = 13/28 (A1)

questions.text:
A manufacturer claims that the mean weight of bags of sugar is 1.00 kg. A quality control inspector tests a random sample of 25 bags and finds the sample mean is 0.985 kg and the sample standard deviation is 0.03 kg.

Test, at the 5% significance level, whether there is evidence that the mean weight is less than 1.00 kg. State your hypotheses and conclusion clearly.
questions.markScheme:
1. H₀: μ = 1.00, H₁: μ < 1.00 (one-tailed test) (M1)
2. Test statistic: t = (0.985 - 1.00)/(0.03/√25) = -0.015/0.006 = -2.5 (M1A1)
3. Critical value at 5% one-tailed with 24 degrees of freedom: t₂₄ = -1.711 (M1)
4. Since -2.5 < -1.711, the test statistic is in the critical region (A1)
5. Reject H₀. There is sufficient evidence at the 5% significance level to conclude that the mean weight is less than 1.00 kg.

questions.text:
A car accelerates uniformly from rest to a speed of 20 m s⁻¹ in 8 seconds, then travels at a constant speed of 20 m s⁻¹ for 12 seconds, before decelerating uniformly to rest in 5 seconds.

(a) Sketch a velocity-time graph for this journey.
(b) Calculate the total distance travelled.
questions.markScheme:
(a) Correct v-t graph with three phases: triangle from (0,0) to (8,20), rectangle from (8,20) to (20,20), triangle from (20,20) to (25,0) (M1)

(b)
2. Distance = area under graph (M1)
3. = ½(8)(20) + (12)(20) + ½(5)(20) (M1)
4. = 80 + 240 + 50 = 370 m (A1)

questions.text:
A particle of mass 4 kg is on a smooth inclined plane angled at 30° to the horizontal. A force P acts on the particle along the plane, directed up the slope.

The particle is in equilibrium.

Calculate the magnitude of P. Take g = 9.8 m s⁻².
questions.markScheme:
1. Resolve parallel to the plane: P = mg sin 30° (M1)
2. P = 4 × 9.8 × sin 30° (M1)
3. P = 4 × 9.8 × 0.5 = 19.6 N (A1)

questions.text:
A ball is projected vertically upwards from ground level with an initial speed of 24.5 m s⁻¹. Take g = 9.8 m s⁻².

(a) Find the maximum height reached by the ball.
(b) Find the total time the ball is in the air before it returns to ground level.

A uniform beam AB of length 6 m and mass 20 kg is supported by two vertical ropes attached at points C and D, where AC = 1 m and AD = 5 m. A box of mass 10 kg is placed on the beam at point A.

Find the tension in each rope. Take g = 9.8 m s⁻².
questions.markScheme:
1. Let Tc = tension at C and Td = tension at D. Take moments about C. (M1)
2. Weight of beam acts at centre (3 m from A, so 2 m from C): 20 × 9.8 × 2 = 392 N m (M1)
3. Weight of box acts at A (1 m from C, on opposite side): 10 × 9.8 × 1 = 98 N m (anticlockwise) (M1)
4. Td × 4 = 392 - 98 = 294, so Td = 73.5 N (A1)
5. Resolving vertically: Tc + Td = (20 + 10) × 9.8 = 294, so Tc = 294 - 73.5 = 220.5 N (A1)
Alternative moment methods accepted.
